Alternative algorithm for deconvoluted gene expression

only cell proportions available from Spatial Deconvolution
Bayes Prism - state of the art algorithm
Iga will update me if she comes up with something better

FAP,PDGFRA, and PDGFRB in HGSC

PDGFRB and PDGFRA:
Some studies report that high stromal PDGFRB correlates with poor prognosis, but findings are inconsistent.
Based on IHC of PDGFRB and PDGFRA in stromal and tumor compartments in HGSC, PDGFRA and PDGFRB doesn’t have prognostic benefit in HGSC [1]. 
FAP:
High FAP⁺ fibroblast abundance alone was not associated with shorter survival in the whole cohort [2].
However, when both CD8⁺ T-cell density and FAP⁺ fibroblast levels were high, outcomes were significantly worse:
In the discovery cohort: 
High FAP + high CD8 → shorter PFS (11.4 vs. 18.6 months, p = 0.01) 
In the validation cohort: 
High FAP + high CD8 → shorter OS (31.5 vs. 76.9 months, p = 0.02)

Madsen, C. V., Steffensen, K. D., Waldstrøm, M., & Jakobsen, A. (2012). Immunohistochemical expression of platelet-derived growth factor receptors in ovarian cancer patients with long-term follow-up. Pathology Research International, 2012, 851432. https://doi.org/10.1155/2012/851432
Corvigno, S., Fernebro, J., Karlsson, J. S., Mezheieusky, A., Martín-Bernabé, A., Martin De La Fuente, L., ... Dahlstrand, H. (2025). High prevalence of FAP+ cancer-associated fibroblasts predicts poor outcome in patients with high-grade serous ovarian cancer with high CD8 T-cell density. Gynecologic Oncology, 193, 148–155. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ygyno.2025.01.010

Is using spatial data suitable for our purposes?

the ROIs in the GeoMX selected based on the immune cell presence - bias in immune cell composition in the ROIs
Batch 1 comes from TSI, while Batch 2 has ROIs from stroma, tumor and TSI
T cells migrate in clusters - T cell abundance in ROIs might be overestimated

Which stromal cell abundances have prognostic benefit (based on PFS and OS)

based on the distribution of cell proportion in stromal segments in different PFS groups, none of the cell types have prognostic significance in HGSC. (macrophages p=0.1)
based on the distribution of cell proportion in stromal segments in different OS groups, Fibroblasts and DCs may be having prognostic significance in HGSC.

Conclusions

-remove several micrometers zone around the tumor AOIs?
-which one of the algorithms is better for the deconvolution (looks like for the tumor cells none of them)
-BP seems good in “amplifying” tumor signal
-SD seems good in “amplifying” immune signal
